w: Set up Polymath: project.yaml + onboarding notes.
Root-cause a reported bug and plan the fix.
Upgrade one dependency safely and fix call-sites.
Frame a hard decision, govern it, record an ADR.
Pressure-test a plan and emit a revised one.
Multi-quarter, usage-gated capability removal. Not the simple two-stage notice-then-remove — use sunsetCapability for that.
Design a system: arch doc + threat model + ADR.
Scope a clear goal — work breakdown, estimate, plan.
A/B + flag rollout from pre-registration to GA.
Discovery (interviews/persona) then ship a feature.
Turn a vague goal into an estimated, mapped plan.
Turn a postmortem into filed action tickets.
Phased migration to a new language/runtime version.
Fix a perf regression and verify it recovered.
Ship from an existing PRD — acceptance through PR draft.
Flag rollout with SLO health gates (no A/B).
Pin behavior in tests, then refactor safely.
PRD to a decomposed, groomed, ready backlog.
Drive a live incident end-to-end to postmortem.
Multi-critic review of a PR/diff + synthesis.
Critique an existing plan; findings only, no rewrite.
Drive to a system root cause; write an RCA.
Fix a security finding end-to-end and verify.
Ship a small feature from PRD to PR draft.
Two-stage sunset: notice + deprecate, remove later. Not usage-gated — use deprecationToRemoval for multi-quarter, usage-gated removal.
Cut a release: notes, verify, tag PR.
